title: Gone to Guf Back for Final Love Gen and Fuyu
display_title: Gone to Guf Back for Final Love Gen and Fuyu
wikitext:
[]: Gone to Guf. Back for Final. -- Love Gen & Fuyu             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri May 10, 2013 7:07 pm Unspoilered Title [SPOILERS] Gone to Guf. Back for Final. -- Love Gen & Fuyu I've started this thread to begin a discussion on what happens to Gendo, Fuyutsuki, and indeed Nerv HQ at the end of 3.33. The last we see of our intrepid pair, they are standing in what looks to be a chamber similar to the Wunder's bridge, as noted by Baz in the Geofront/Moon thread (By the way, the images in this post are all on the wiki, and are posted here using the wiki image integration system , which is super useful. Click on any image to be taken to a larger version on the wiki proper. Thanks to Reichu for uploading these images to the Wiki as well. ) [wkimg width=400]Eva3-33 C1479 gf-bridge.jpg[/wkimg] But where are they? And where is Nerv HQ? All we can see is that the chamber is black all about them. However, if we return to the Wunder bridge startup sequence, you can see that in its "inactive" state, the Wunder chamber is initially a dull red, similar to standard entry plugs. Only later does the monitor become active. Wunder Bridge LCL uncharged/charged  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri May 10, 2013 7:36 pm I've never thought about where the Chamber of Guf led to before. Now that I'm thinking about it, I would guess that they are in a Dirac Sea-esque space. Once again, since that area would be so big, nothing would be appearing on screens. As to their exact location, I would guess the obvious; this Neo-Nerv got sucked into Guf during the sequence where Mari deactivates Evangelion-13. Of course, what Gendo and Fuyutsuki are going to do now is anybody's idea. The only things that we know exist inside of the funky pyramid are this Wunder-like bridge and some construction equipment (see scene w/ Kaworu, Shinji, and the splatchy moon.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 10, 2013 7:54 pm This is one messed-up thread... I like it! OMF: The Chamber of Guf, or whatever it is, only looks different in the last shot because of perspective. There's a cross sticking out of the very bottom, and the four arms of the cross are what you're seeing. Nuclear Lunchbox: The pyramid contains all of Nerv's current facilities, it's implied.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri May 10, 2013 8:17 pm Reichu wrote: Nuclear Lunchbox: The pyramid contains all of Nerv's current facilities, it's implied.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri May 10, 2013 8:34 pm Reichu wrote: Nuclear Lunchbox: The pyramid contains all of Nerv's current facilities, it's implied.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri May 10, 2013 8:35 pm ElMariachi wrote: Wait, are you talking about the giant inverted pyramid or the ruins of NERV's pyramid?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat May 11, 2013 12:57 am Seeing as the Guf is a seemingly limitless parallel dimension, I guess it could make sense that we can't see the pyramid inside. It may have been moved further into the Guf along those red thread-like things. What I'm wondering about is whether the red threads were caused by something in Nerv HQ (that is to say, whatever was producing them was outside the Guf and then moved all of Nerv and itself inside) or whether Nerv was getting drawn into the Guf by something that was already inside (say, for example, a Dark!Wunder or the white ufo-thing). It also seems strange that Gendo and Fuyu would actually want to be in the Guf, unless there's something important (Dark!Wunder, or possibly the white ufo and crosses?) that they want to locate. Not really sure how this relates to the "reunite with Yui plan". I'm also not sure how they're going to get out until another Impact occurs, seeing as the Door of Guf closed. It seems that the Guf is no longer an empty container of souls like it was in NGE; rather, it seems that physical objects and people from outside the Guf can now move inside it and survive to some extent. It seems there are objects already in the Guf, although it was empty in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat May 11, 2013 2:58 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Seeing as the Guf is a seemingly limitless parallel dimension, I guess it could make sense that we can't see the pyramid inside.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Gone to ---. Back for Final. -- Love --- &amp             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat May 11, 2013 4:07 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'd like to propose that Gendo and Fuyutsuki have in fact gone through into the Guf .

[]:             Quote Post by Mt Olympus » Sat May 11, 2013 4:13 am I'm not sure if you were alluding to this but I don't recall seeing the white moon anywhere during the fourth impact perhaps that was it.This is a very good thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat May 11, 2013 4:22 am I haven't seen anybody talking about a white moon in this thread. Do we even know if it still exists? edit: You mean the sphere could be the white moon?

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Gone to ---. Back for Final. -- Love --- &amp             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat May 11, 2013 4:33 am Jinroh wrote: wat. It's the weirdest things I've read in ages.

[]:             Quote Post by Mt Olympus » Sat May 11, 2013 4:34 am Yes, I was thinking that maybe they are using the moon base or their own newly built HQ on the white moon.The moon also seemed to be floating not far off of earth's surface, that could be a testament to the antigravity idea.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Gone to ---. Back for Final. -- Love --- &amp             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat May 11, 2013 5:23 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Seeing as the Guf is a seemingly limitless parallel dimension, I guess it could make sense that we can't see the pyramid inside.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Gone to ---. Back for Final. -- Love --- &amp             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat May 11, 2013 5:52 am Jinroh wrote: fully raise the black moon

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat May 11, 2013 5:53 am Why would he want to go in there? Nothing's shown to enter the Guf, Shinji would have seen it. And it doesn't seem to be possible to open the door without initiating an impact. Supposing Gendo entered the Guf only because he's in a dark room is far fetched imo... But now that Seele failed to bring instrumentality, black moon is useless as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat May 11, 2013 5:58 am Jinroh wrote: Why would it be useless for Gendo's plans and how can you deduce that knowing we have no information relative to that particular topic yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat May 11, 2013 6:01 am But if you want to speculate, you need to go through a process in which you find some arguments to defend your theories, otherwise they come out of nowhere. You can't just straight out say the blackmoon is useless...

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat May 11, 2013 6:03 am Jinroh wrote: You can't just straight out say the blackmoon is useless...

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat May 11, 2013 6:06 am I didn't say it was useful. I am just saying you can't pretend it's useless without information regarding that particular topic. Do you get my point? edit: Fair enough. It's gonna be a long and frustrating wait :(
